message.content:
Research the shareholding pattern and ownership structure for Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd, an Indian EMS/defense-electronics company (NSE: VINNY, BSE: 542907) based in Mysore.

Use WebSearch and WebFetch on screener.in (https://www.screener.in/company/VINNY/), trendlyne.com, tickertape.in, moneycontrol.com, and BSE/NSE shareholding disclosures.

Find and report with inline source URLs and the QUARTER/DATE for each figure:
1. Latest quarter shareholding pattern: promoter holding %, FII/FPI %, DII %, public %.
2. Promoter pledge % (shares pledged) - is any promoter stake pledged?
3. Trend over the last 4-6 quarters - is promoter holding rising or falling? Are FIIs/DIIs increasing stake?
4. Names of key promoters / major shareholders / notable institutional investors.
5. Any recent insider buying/selling, or changes in promoter stake.

Be precise on the quarter (e.g., Mar 2026, Dec 2025) for each figure. Return findings as a concise markdown section with source URLs. Do NOT write any files - return findings directly as text.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Latest Shareholding Pattern

The shareholding pattern of Vinyas Innovative Technologies is as follows: DIIs - 4.53%, FII - 0.00%, Promoters - 29.39%, and Public Investors 50.57%.

Alternatively, another source reports slightly different figures: Promoter held 29.4%, the FII held 0.5%, DII held 3.5% and the public held 66.6% of the total shares.

## Key Observations:

- **Promoter Holding**: Promoter Holding: 29.4%
- **FII (Foreign Institutional Investors)**: Minimal presence at 0-0.5%
- **DII (Domestic Institutional Investors)**: 3.5-4.53%
- **Public/Non-Institution**: Majority stakeholder at 50-66%

The shareholding pattern shows that promoters maintain a significant stake of approximately 29%, while the majority of shares are held by public investors. Foreign institutional investors have minimal participation in the company.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies - Shareholding Pattern

## Quarterly Shareholding (as percentages):

| Quarter | Promoters | FIIs | DIIs | Public | Government | Shareholders |
|---------|-----------|------|------|--------|------------|--------------|
| Mar 2024 | 29.38% | 0.03% | 3.24% | 67.35% | — | 854 |
| Jun 2024 | 29.38% | 0.03% | 2.77% | 67.82% | — | 1,066 |
| Sep 2024 | 29.38% | 0.03% | 2.75% | 67.84% | — | 1,478 |
| Mar 2025 | 29.39% | 0.48% | 3.51% | 66.62% | — | 1,332 |
| Sep 2025 | 29.39% | 0.47% | 4.53% | 65.61% | — | 1,551 |
| Mar 2026 | 29.40% | 0.49% | 6.53% | 63.59% | — | 1,673 |

## Key Observations:

- **Promoter holding** remains relatively stable at approximately 29.4% throughout the period
- **Foreign institutional investor (FII) participation** increased from 0.03% to 0.49%
- **Domestic institutional investor (DII) holdings** grew notably from 3.24% to 6.53%
- **Public shareholding** decreased correspondingly from 67.35% to 63.59%
- **No promoter pledge data** is disclosed in the provided section
- **Government shareholding** shows no separate listing in the data

Shareholder count increased from 854 to 1,673 over the two-year period.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies - Latest Shareholding Pattern

**Latest Quarter: Mar 2026**

| Category | Percentage |
|----------|-----------|
| Promoter Holding | 29.4% |
| Promoter Pledge | 0.0% |
| FII/FPI | 0.5% |
| DII | 6.5% |
| Mutual Funds | 0.0% |
| Public/Retail | 63.6% |

**Promoter Locked-in Shares:** 68.05%

---

## Shareholding Trends (Last 6 Quarters)

**Promoter Holding:** Stable at 29.4% across all periods (Mar 2024 - Mar 2026)

**FII/FPI:** Increased from 0.0% (Jun 2024, Sep 2024, Mar 2025) to 0.5% (Sep 2025, Mar 2026)

**DII:** Steady upward trajectory from 2.8% (Sep 2024) → 3.2% (Mar 2024) → 3.5% (Mar 2025) → 4.5% (Sep 2025) → 6.5% (Mar 2026)

**Public Holding:** Declining from 67.4% (Mar 2024) to 63.6% (Mar 2026)

---

## Key Promoters

The primary promoters are individuals/HUF members:
- **Narendra Narayanan:** 18.51% (99.99% locked)
- **Meera Narendra:** 10.87% (13.80% locked)
- Other family members hold minimal stakes (<0.01%)

---

## Notable Activity

No significant recent buying/selling by promoters reported. The increase in DII holdings appears to reflect institutional accumulation rather than promotional changes.

content.text:
I have enough data to produce a thorough report. The data from screener.in (VINYAS) and Trendlyne corroborate each other well. Let me compile the findings.

---

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE: VINYAS / BSE: 542907) — Shareholding & Ownership

**Note on tickers:** The screener.in URL `screener.in/company/VINNY/` actually points to an unrelated company ("Vinny Overseas Ltd"). The correct screener page for Vinyas is **[screener.in/company/VINYAS/](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/)**. All figures below are for the correct company (CIN/Trendlyne ID 1715908).

## 1. Latest quarter shareholding (Mar 2026 / Q4 FY26)
Sources: [Trendlyne](https://trendlyne.com/equity/share-holding/1715908/VINYAS/latest/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/), [Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/)

| Category | Mar 2026 |
|---|---|
| Promoter | 29.40% |
| FII / FPI | ~0.49% |
| DII | 6.53% |
| Public / Retail | 63.59% |
| Government | nil |

(Mutual funds within DII ~0.0% per Trendlyne; DII is mostly other domestic institutions/NBFC/insurance type holders.)

## 2. Promoter pledge
**0.0% pledged** — no promoter shares are pledged (Mar 2026). Source: [Trendlyne](https://trendlyne.com/equity/share-holding/1715908/VINYAS/latest/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/). Separately, **~68% of promoter shares are locked-in** (regulatory lock-in, not a pledge), led by Narendra Narayanan's stake being 99.99% locked-in.

## 3. Trend over the last ~6 quarters
Source: [Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/) and [Trendlyne](https://trendlyne.com/equity/share-holding/1715908/VINYAS/latest/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/)

| Quarter | Promoter | FII | DII | Public |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mar 2024 | 29.38% | 0.03% | 3.24% | 67.35% |
| Jun 2024 | 29.38% | 0.03% | 2.77% | 67.82% |
| Sep 2024 | 29.38% | 0.03% | 2.75% | 67.84% |
| Mar 2025 | 29.39% | 0.48% | 3.51% | 66.62% |
| Sep 2025 | 29.39% | 0.47% | 4.53% | 65.61% |
| Mar 2026 | 29.40% | 0.49% | 6.53% | 63.59% |

- **Promoter holding: flat/stable** at ~29.4% throughout (no dilution, no significant buying).
- **FII: rising slightly** off a near-zero base — entered around Mar 2025 (~0.5%) and held steady.
- **DII: clear upward accumulation** — roughly doubled from ~2.8–3.2% to **6.53%** over two years; this is the standout institutional trend.
- **Public/retail: declining** from ~67% to ~63.6%, the mirror of DII accumulation.

(Intermediate quarters Dec 2024, Jun 2025, Dec 2025 weren't separately captured in the fetched tables but fall within these trend lines.)

## 4. Key promoters / major shareholders
Source: [Trendlyne](https://trendlyne.com/equity/share-holding/1715908/VINYAS/latest/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/), [SimplyWall.st](https://simplywall.st/stocks/in/tech/nse-vinyas/vinyas-innovative-technologies-shares)
- **Narendra Narayanan** — Chairman/CEO and largest promoter, ~18.5–19% (single biggest shareholder; ~100% locked-in).
- **Meera Narendra** — ~10.87%.
- Other promoter-group names: Narendra Sumukh, T. R. Srinivasan, Pradeep Vithoba Desai, Deepa Prakash, B S Ramakrishna Mudre, Subodh M R (small/family stakes).
- Institutional side is small (<5–7% total). Specific DII fund/scheme names were not disclosed in the aggregated sources fetched; for exact institutional holder names you'd need the BSE/NSE shareholding-pattern PDF filings (BSE code 542907).

## 5. Recent insider/promoter activity
- **No material promoter buying or selling** reported — promoter stake has been essentially constant (~29.4%) and unpledged.
- The notable ownership change is **DII accumulation** (3% → 6.5%), i.e., institutional buying from the open market rather than any promoter-driven change.

**Caveats:** Aggregator figures occasionally differ by a few basis points (e.g., FII shown as 0.0% vs 0.49% depending on rounding/quarter). For audited precision and named institutional holders, the authoritative source is the company's quarterly Reg-31 shareholding-pattern filing on BSE (scrip 542907) / NSE (VINYAS).

Sources:
- [Screener — Vinyas (VINYAS)](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/)
- [Trendlyne — latest shareholding](https://trendlyne.com/equity/share-holding/1715908/VINYAS/latest/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/)
- [Trendlyne — Mar 2025 shareholding](https://trendlyne.com/equity/share-holding/1715908/VINYAS/31-03-2025/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/)
- [SimplyWall.st — Vinyas ownership](https://simplywall.st/stocks/in/tech/nse-vinyas/vinyas-innovative-technologies-shares)
